headline: Multi-chain vault protocol with limited address discovery; core architecture documented but deployed contracts not fully enumerable
short_headline: Vault infrastructure across 11 chains; incomplete deployment catalog

findings.text: Website fetch (veda.tech) confirms Veda is a DeFi vault provider offering enterprise-grade yield products. No on-site contract directory.
Documentation site docs.veda.tech contains architecture and audit pages. No dedicated deployed-contracts directory found at standard paths (/deployments, /contracts, /addresses).

Audits page (docs.veda.tech/security-and-risk-controls/audits.md) lists 15 completed audits from 0xMacro and Spearbit covering BoringVault Arctic architecture, teller, accountant, queue, solver, and decoder components. Audit scope documents not fetched.

GitHub repo Se7en-Seas/boring-vault contains /deployments folder (not directly inspected due to access restrictions). README documents BoringVault, Manager, Teller, Accountant, and Arctic Architecture components.

Protocol targets 11 chains (Ethereum, Ink, Hyperliquid L1, Plasma, Base, Sonic, Arbitrum, Berachain, Binance, BOB, Scroll). Block explorer enumeration per chain not attempted due to fetch budget constraints.
One vault discovered via Etherscan search: weETHs (0x917cee801a67f933f2e6b33fc0cd1ed2d5909d88) on Ethereum, verified as BoringVault contract. Factory pattern / child enumeration not performed.

Core contract roles from architecture docs: BoringVault (fund custody), Manager (strategy whitelisting via Merkle tree), Teller (user deposits/withdrawals), Accountant (exchange rate provision), Queue/Solver (withdrawal processing), DecoderAndSanitizer (calldata validation).
Discovered addresses: weETHs vault on Ethereum (0x917cee801a67f933f2e6b33fc0cd1ed2d5909d88) cited from Etherscan search result. No multisig, timelock, or admin addresses extracted.

rationale.verdict: Discovery incomplete. Veda is a multi-chain vault infrastructure with documented architecture and multiple audits, but no canonical deployed-contracts list found. One vault (weETHs) discovered on Ethereum via Etherscan. Full address enumeration across 11 chains and identification of governance/admin roles requires access to GitHub deployment artifacts or chain-specific searches. Protocol metadata refreshed with available documentation URLs; admin_addresses empty pending deployment discovery.

evidence.shows: Protocol homepage confirming Veda as onchain vault provider with enterprise-grade security and multi-chain capability. No contract addresses listed.

Documentation overview describing BoringVault as a vault primitive with non-custodial funds, Merkle-verified strategies, and modular architecture (BoringVault, Manager, Teller, Accountant components).

Audit index listing 15 completed audits: Full Architecture audit (0xMacro A-4, Spearbit), Accountant/Teller updates (A-8, A-36, A-45), Decoder/Sanitizer contracts (A-9, A-10, A-15, A-18, A-19, A-38, A-44), Queue/Solver (A-18, A-25, A-27, A-38, A-44). No deployed contract addresses in this document.

Main repository for BoringVault with /deployments folder present (not fetched). README describes architecture: BoringVault (barebones custody), Manager (Merkle-verified strategies), Teller (deposits/withdrawals), Accountant (exchange rates), Arctic Architecture variant.

Super Symbiotic LRT (weETHs) vault at 0x917cee801a67f933f2e6b33fc0cd1ed2d5909d88 on Ethereum. Contract: BoringVault (v0.8.21+commit.d9974bed). Description: automated vault earning Symbiotic, Veda and ether.fi points on liquid staking assets.

unknowns: D1: Block explorer name-tag search not performed for all 11 chains (Ethereum, Ink, Hyperliquid L1, Plasma, Base, Sonic, Arbitrum, Berachain, Binance, BOB, Scroll) due to fetch budget
D2: Canonical deployed-contracts doc not found; tried /deployments, /addresses, /contracts, /getting-started/deployments paths without success
D4: GitHub deployments folder structure not fetched; access restricted or requires directory listing
D5: Per-chain block explorer enumeration incomplete; would require 11 separate explorer searches and likely factory contract enumeration
D6: Factory pattern (if used) and child vault enumeration not attempted; would require reading Manager or factory contract state via on-chain read API
D7-D8: Governance, multisig, timelock, and owner/admin roles not extracted; would require reading live contract state (owner(), getOwners(), authority(), etc.) or audit PDF scope tables

protocol_metadata.about: Veda is a DeFi vault primitive providing non-custodial, multi-chain infrastructure for yield optimization. The BoringVault enables developers to package complex DeFi strategies into audited vault contracts with onchain Merkle-verified operational constraints. Users deposit assets through a Teller contract and receive vault shares; funds are custodied in the BoringVault and deployed according to curator-submitted rebalance messages verified against a Merkle tree whitelist.
